meaning: Taxi onto the runway, align with the centerline, and wait — takeoff clearance not yet issued.
simpleMeaning: Enter the runway, line up, and wait for takeoff clearance.
portugueseMeaning: Entrar na pista, alinhar e aguardar autorização de decolagem.
operationalMeaning: You are on the runway but not cleared for takeoff — position for imminent departure.
whoUsesIt: Tower controllers; pilots read back and comply exactly.
whenUsed: When departure is imminent but landing traffic or wake turbulence requires a short wait.
whyUsed: Reduces taxi time while keeping the runway protected until takeoff is safe.

realExamples.text: ANAC 123, runway three six, line up and wait.
realExamples.speaker: Tower

realExamples.text: Behind the landing Airbus, line up and wait runway one eight.
realExamples.speaker: Tower

realExamples.text: Line up and wait runway three six, ANAC 123.
realExamples.speaker: Pilot

pilotResponses: Line up and wait runway three six, ANAC 123.
Holding in position runway three six, line up and wait, ANAC 123.
icaoQuestions: Why is a correct readback critical after 'line up and wait'?
What must you not do after lining up and waiting?
commonMistakes: Taking off without hearing 'cleared for takeoff'.
Confusing line up and wait with position and hold.
Incomplete readback missing the runway number.
pronunciationTips: LINE UP AND WAIT — four distinct words, steady pace.
Do not clip 'and' — it matters legally.

compareTerms.note: Takeoff clearance follows line up and wait — they are not the same.
Hold short keeps you off the runway; line up and wait puts you on it.

didYouKnow: ICAO phraseology replaced 'position and hold' with 'line up and wait'.
captainStory: Morning rush at Congonhas, Tower sequenced three departures with line up and wait — each crew waited for takeoff clearance before adding power.

references.title: ICAO Delta Tower Phraseology — Line Up and Wait
